FACTS
The San Bernardino County Sheriff's Departnent has
implernented a rigorous and extensive volunteer proqram. According
to your facts, reserve police officers, citizen patrol officers,
and other unpaid staff of the departnent perforn general 1aw
enforcement dutj.es, either in conjunction with, or independent of
salaried officers. 
The rnaterials you subrnitted describe sone of
the functions that the unpaid staff perform, such as riding in
patrol cars, traffic control', crowd control and search and rescue'
unpaid staff also use agency equipment and vehicles.
Because of the substantial involvement that unpaid staff
members have in performing the Law enforcement duties of the
department, you Stated that the unpaid staff require the. same
IeveI of tra-ining and informatj.on dissemination as salaried
employees. your materj.als indicate that the unpaid staff members
arL tiained prior to performing services, and have regular
periodic meetings. In addition, the unpaid staff nembers are
requi.red to follow the rules and regul'ations of the department,
Lnil.uaing those concerning the treatment of citizens'
You have asked whether unpaid staff may be treated as paid
staff pursuant to the exception in subdivision (b) (4) of
Regulation 18901. You have submitted ti/o types.of samples:.
coirnunications fron the sheriff concernj'ng the internal \',orkings
of the department, including promotions, policies -on racism, and
reorganizltion of unj'ts withi.n the departnent; and, the Scope, a
newsietter to keep the staff of the department up to date on the
sheriff/s policies and goals. The sample you submitted had a
message frtm the sheriff, a Ietters column, and articles on-
variotjs Iaw enforcement issues such as the imp)'ementation of
bicycle officer patrols in the county.
ANALYSIS
In June 1988 Proposition 73 amended Section 89001- of the Act
to provide: "No newsLetter or other mass mailj'ng shall be sent at
puOtic expense. " A literal 
reading of this section led to the
tcnclusion that al.I mass rnailings involving public funds,
irrespective of content or purpose, were prohj'bited by section
89001. fn response to a variety of questions concerning the
dj.stribution of tax notices, tax refund checks, community college
schedules, sample ba11ots, and other mass mailings customarily
sent by government agencies, the Commiss j,on adopted Regulation

be exempted from the
restrj.ctions of the Regulation 18901. Thus, the conrmunications

sections.question: Are intra-agency communications which are sent in the normal course of business to employees, officers, deputies, and paid and unpaid staff members of the San Bernardino County Sheriff's Department subject to the restrictions of the Act?
sections.conclusion: Since the unpaid staff members of San Bernardino County are similar to salaried officers, internal administrative communications may also be sent to unpaid staff and are exempt from the restrictions of Regulation 18901.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether intra-agency communications sent to both salaried employees and unpaid volunteer staff in the normal course of business are subject to political practices restrictions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Unpaid volunteer staff members of the sheriff's department may receive the same intra-agency communications as salaried employees without triggering mass mailing restrictions under Regulation 18901, provided the conditions regarding content, distribution method, and public expense are met.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document has clear Q/C sections with some OCR scanning artifacts in the header/footer areas. The question and conclusion sections are identifiable despite formatting issues. Extensive regulatory analysis follows the conclusion section. Document appears complete.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are intra-agency communications which are sent in the normal course of business to employees, officers, deputies, and paid and unpaid staff members of the San Bernardino County Sheriff's Department subject to the restrictions of the Act?

CONCLUSION: Since the unpaid staff members of San Bernardino County are similar to salaried officers, internal administrative communications may also be sent to unpaid staff and are exempt from the restrictions of Regulation 18901.

embedding.summary: This advice letter addresses whether the San Bernardino County Sheriff's Department can distribute internal communications to both salaried employees and unpaid volunteer staff members without violating mass mailing restrictions under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC concludes that unpaid staff can receive such communications under certain conditions, while providing detailed analysis of when newsletters and mass mailings trigger regulatory restrictions.
